title: hieracium prenanthoides subsp. polyphaeum
intro: Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. polyphaeum, also known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is commonly found in mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used topically in the form of salves or creams.
